questions.question: Which prompt will get a better answer?
questions.instruction: Click the option you think is better:

options.hint: Short. Simple. Straight to the point.

options.text: "You are a CS tutor. I am a non-technical product manager. Explain machine learning in 3 bullet points using everyday examples."
options.hint: Includes Role, Context, Task, and Format.

breakdown.label: R — Role
breakdown.text: "You are a CS tutor" → The LLM knows what expertise to use.

breakdown.label: C — Context
breakdown.text: "non-technical product manager" → It knows your level and background.

breakdown.label: F — Format
breakdown.text: "3 bullet points, everyday examples" → You get exactly what you asked for.

explanation.note: Option A ("explain machine learning") gives the LLM nothing to work with — no level, no format, no context. You get a generic textbook definition.

questions.question: What's missing from this prompt?
questions.prompt_quote: "Write me a Python function that parses JSON and returns a list of user names."

options.text: Role — no instruction on what expertise level to use
Context — no info about JSON structure, project, or constraints
Format — no spec for error handling, type hints, or docs

explanation.summary: Correct answer: D — All of the above

breakdown.label: Missing Role
breakdown.text: Should the LLM act as a senior dev? A data engineer? We don't know.

breakdown.label: Missing Context
breakdown.text: What is the JSON structure? Flat dict? Nested? What project is this for?

breakdown.label: Missing Format
breakdown.text: Should it include type hints? Docstrings? Error handling for malformed JSON?

explanation.note: Improved: "You are a Python expert. I am writing a data pipeline. Parse a JSON list of user objects {name, email} and return a list of names. Include type hints and a try/except block."

questions.question: Which is the best improvement to this prompt?

options.hint: Slightly more specific, still very vague.

options.text: "You are a Python expert. I have AttributeError: NoneType has no attribute 'split' on line 23 of my Flask app. Fix in under 5 lines."
options.hint: Full RCTF: Role, Context, Task, Format.

options.text: "Please help debug my Python code where I get a NoneType error"
options.hint: More polite, but no role, no exact error, no format.

explanation.summary: Option B is the only prompt that uses all 4 parts of RCTF:

breakdown.label: C — Context
breakdown.text: "AttributeError: NoneType… on line 23 of my Flask app"

questions.question: Identify the RCTF components in this prompt:
questions.instruction: For each label, select which sentence it maps to:
questions.prompt_quote: "You are a senior data scientist. I'm a business analyst who's never used Python before. Write a script that reads a CSV and plots a bar chart of monthly sales. Use comments to explain each line and keep it under 20 lines."

sentences.text: You are a senior data scientist.
I'm a business analyst who's never used Python before.
Write a script that reads a CSV and plots a bar chart of monthly sales.
Use comments to explain each line and keep it under 20 lines.

explanation.summary: Here's how the prompt breaks down:

breakdown.label: C — Context
breakdown.text: "I'm a business analyst who's never used Python before."

breakdown.label: T — Task
breakdown.text: "Write a script that reads a CSV and plots a bar chart of monthly sales."

breakdown.label: F — Format
breakdown.text: "Use comments to explain each line and keep it under 20 lines."

explanation.note: Each sentence does exactly one job. When your prompt has all 4, the LLM has everything it needs to give a great answer.

questions.question: Which part of RCTF is this prompt missing?
questions.prompt_quote: "Act as a senior editor. My blog post is about urban gardening for beginners. Rewrite the intro."

options.hint: "Act as a senior editor" — that's already a Role.
"blog post about urban gardening for beginners" — that's Context.
No length, tone, or structure is specified.

breakdown.label: F — Format
breakdown.text: No word count, no tone, no structure — the LLM has to guess.

explanation.note: Better: "Rewrite the intro in 3 sentences, conversational tone, ending with a hook question."
